query: Cost reset under margin and unused-capacity pressure A semiconductor company faces weaker revenue, unused manufacturing capacity, gross-margin pressure, and a balance sheet funding an expensive technology transition. A proposed reset combines operating-cost reductions, lower near-term capital spending, and continued funding for the core process roadmap. How should the CFO allocate reductions without converting a strategic reset into indiscriminate austerity?
